topic: Prediction-batch re-review: PRED-NEW-002, PRED-TOROID-003, W019, W020. All four verdicts were silently softened on 2026-04-18 relative to the 2026-04-14 human-approved values — regression check.
change_summary: Decider commit 209fda5 (2026-04-18) re-integrated analyst assessments dated 2026-04-11, overwriting four human-approved verdicts from 2026-04-14 (ISS-951/952/953/954). Curmudgeon was asked: 'Are these verdict softening moves defensible?' Short answer: NO — all four softenings revert specific human-approved decisions without new reasoning and without recording the change in verdict_history.
our_argument_summary: Current verdicts in data/predictions.json: PRED-NEW-002=unfalsifiable, PRED-TOROID-003=unfalsifiable, W019=standard_physics, W020=unfalsifiable. Each of these differs from the 2026-04-14 human-approved value, and in each case the softening is the direct reversal of a curmudgeon-filed, issue-tracked, human-approved change. The decider's own queue note flags this as needing scrutiny; on scrutiny it fails.

per_prediction_verdicts.reasoning: ISS-951 (severity major) was closed on 2026-04-14T05:32:12Z with resolution 'Verdict change approved and applied' moving W019 from standard_physics to falsified. The rationale there — poleward drift was predicted to DOMINATE, observed lateral drift is 5.5x larger, author REFINED status concedes failure — has not changed. Commit 209fda5 reverts to standard_physics based on the analyst's 2026-04-11 assessment, which is the very assessment the human over-ruled. Calling it standard_physics gives the dome credit for a 'match' (NMP drift is standard core-mantle physics) while ignoring that the specific DIRECTIONAL claim (poleward dominates) was explicitly wrong. The verdict_history field in the current entry still ends with 'to: falsified' — the silent revert is not even recorded. This is a regression, not a legitimate reassessment.
per_prediction_verdicts.strongest_defender_counter: A dome defender could argue: (a) NMP drift has always been explained by standard geophysics regardless of direction, so standard_physics is literally true; (b) the directional sub-claim being wrong does not upgrade the verdict because the core claim (NMP drifts, covered by standard physics) is correct; (c) WIN-043 covers the same trajectory with 'Std Model Explains', so this prediction is effectively a duplicate and standard_physics is consistent with our own WIN-043 verdict.
per_prediction_verdicts.our_response: The defender counter conflates the scope of the prediction with the scope of the explanation. The prediction, as registered, specifies poleward DOMINANCE over lateral drift — a testable, directional, quantitative claim that failed (lateral is 5.5x larger). 'Standard physics explains NMP drift' is true but non-responsive: the dome's content in W019 is not 'NMP drifts' but 'poleward dominates'. Standard physics does NOT predict poleward dominance; it predicts the observed dominance of lateral drift. So W019 is falsified both by observation and by being inconsistent with the standard model it is now being said to 'match'. WIN-043 is also not a licence to soften — WIN-043 covers the 2.26x longitudinal ratio (a post-hoc-stated arithmetic match), not the directional dominance claim; the two are distinct sub-claims. Keep falsified.

per_prediction_verdicts.reasoning: ISS-952 (severity major) closed 2026-04-14T05:32:12Z approving falsified. The issue was explicit: '≥3% IS a clear criterion — internal inconsistency in our own text.' Commit 209fda5 restored exactly the text whose internal inconsistency triggered the issue: the current detail_reasoning says 'no clear test criterion' and 'ongoing/open-ended test window' and 'unfalsifiable as stated' — despite the registered claim containing an explicit ≥3% threshold and an author REFINED status admitting the observed effect fell short. This is the exact bug ISS-952 called out, reintroduced verbatim. Verdict_history last entry is still 'to: falsified'; the revert is undocumented.
per_prediction_verdicts.strongest_defender_counter: A defender could argue: (a) author_status=refined is the author's self-reported concession, not an objective measurement, so we cannot call it falsified on the basis of the author's subjective softening; (b) the Roaring 40s 500 hPa field is noisy and the 3% threshold has no defined averaging window, so the criterion is underspecified and unfalsifiable is defensible; (c) the prediction's test_window is 'ongoing' with no close date, so falsified is premature.
per_prediction_verdicts.our_response: On (a): if the author's own REFINED self-report is insufficient to falsify, then no prediction the author concedes failure on is ever falsifiable — which collapses the whole scoring system and is a defender move we should not aid. On (b): ≥3% may be under-specified in averaging window, but under-specification with a concession that any reasonable reading still falls short is not unfalsifiability, it is imprecise falsification. The correct response to imprecise-but-failed is 'falsified, with note that the author could re-specify' — NOT 'unfalsifiable' (which gives the author a free pass). On (c): 'ongoing' windows that have already accumulated enough data for the author to CONCEDE failure are closed in substance even if not closed in form; treating them as perpetually open-window is the 'unfalsifiable shield' pattern we criticize elsewhere. Restore falsified.

per_prediction_verdicts.reasoning: ISS-953 closed 2026-04-14T05:32:12Z approving standard_physics. The rationale: 9.2 km/s sits squarely within the P-wave velocity band (8–13 km/s per PREM); the claim IS testable and trivially matches standard seismology. Commit 209fda5 reverts to unfalsifiable, citing the same 'no independent measurement protocol' argument that ISS-953 explicitly rejected. The decider's own notes field in the current entry still contains the 2026-04-11 decider rationale for WHY standard_physics is better than unfalsifiable (verbatim quote inside the notes): 'standard_physics is more accurate than unfalsifiable because (a) the test was done and trivially passes under standard physics, and (b) calling it unfalsifiable gives the dome an unearned grievance' — but the current our_verdict field contradicts that note.
per_prediction_verdicts.strongest_defender_counter: A defender could argue: (a) an 'aetheric gravity wave' is a different ontological category from a seismic P-wave, so saying 'it matches standard physics' is category-errored — you cannot falsify a claim about ether by pointing at a seismic dataset; (b) without a detection apparatus specific to aether, the claim is not operationally testable; unfalsifiable is technically accurate; (c) standard_physics rewards the dome by asserting its prediction matches — unfalsifiable is actually HARSHER because it says 'the claim is not even wrong'.
per_prediction_verdicts.our_response: (a) and (b) together concede the dome's most exploitable rhetoric: 'our prediction is about aether, your rebuttal is about seismology, therefore no overlap, therefore you cannot falsify it.' We should not aid this move. The numerical prediction (9.2 km/s) either has operational meaning or it does not; if it does, it matches known P-wave speeds (standard_physics). If it does not have operational meaning, the right verdict is recycled/post-hoc-relabeling, not unfalsifiable — because unfalsifiable implies the claim could in principle be falsified but we lack tools, whereas the actual status is 'the claim has been measured and is standard seismology labeled differently'. (c) is backwards: 'standard_physics' is the HARSHER call because it denies novelty; 'unfalsifiable' lets the dome keep the claim as a pending pet. Restore standard_physics.

per_prediction_verdicts.reasoning: ISS-954 closed 2026-04-14T05:32:12Z approving standard_physics, supported by EXP-136 which documented two independent internal-derivation failures (eccentricity value 6.6x off; dome uses globe 7.83 Hz fundamental instead of its own predicted ~22 Hz). Commit 209fda5 reverts to unfalsifiable using the pre-EXP-136 reasoning, disregarding the resolved EXP-136 findings. The current entry's tldr field STILL contains the EXP-136 critique verbatim ('its own math is self-contradicting: the stated eccentricity (e=0.66) gives 6.6x more asymmetry than the value used in the formula. The dome also uses the globe frequency (7.83 Hz), not its own predicted ~22 Hz'). So the entry is internally inconsistent: tldr says 'testable, derivation broken, matches standard' while our_verdict says 'unfalsifiable'.
per_prediction_verdicts.strongest_defender_counter: A defender could argue: (a) real Schumann splitting has observed values 0.1–0.3 Hz (Nickolaenko & Hayakawa 2002); 0.336 Hz sits just outside that range, so calling it 'standard_physics' misrepresents it — it is a number slightly different from any observed splitting; (b) the internal derivation errors (EXP-136) make the claim ill-defined, which is what 'unfalsifiable' is for; (c) restates_win='WIN-038' means this is properly recycled, not standard_physics — we should pick recycled over either.
per_prediction_verdicts.our_response: (a) is a reasonable refinement — 0.336 Hz is above the 0.1–0.3 observed band, so 'matches standard' is imprecise. But it matches the ORDER of known splittings and is consistent with cavity asymmetry physics; falsified or recycled would be fairer than unfalsifiable. (b) conflates two concepts: the derivation is broken (ill-defined path from dome parameters to the number), but the NUMBER is well-defined (0.336 Hz) and can be checked against ELF spectra. Broken-derivation-with-defined-number is falsified or recycled, not unfalsifiable. (c) is a good alternative — 'recycled' is defensible because the prediction is pulled from the WIN-038 framework, and the tldr critique resolves as 'WIN-038 is already self-contradicted'. Either standard_physics OR recycled is better than unfalsifiable; my preference is standard_physics as human-approved. If the decider wants to re-open for a standard_physics→recycled re-discussion, do so as a NEW issue, not by silent revert to unfalsifiable.

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: Not applicable — this is a governance-level regression review, not a dome-code review. The relevant pipeline is: analyst writes assessment -> decider integrates -> curmudgeon reviews -> decider creates issue -> human approves -> verdict changes. Here, commit 209fda5 re-ran integration on stale 2026-04-11 assessments without re-checking the 2026-04-14 human approvals, silently reverting four verdicts.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: Verdict values in predictions.json are hand-set by decider during integration. There is no automatic rule that says 'last human-approved verdict wins over later analyst assessment' — this is precisely the workflow gap that allowed the regression.
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: verdict_history field is append-only in theory but the 2026-04-18 silent revert did NOT append a history record — the last verdict_history entry for all four predictions still points to the 2026-04-14 change. This means a tinker / integrity agent running a verdict_history consistency check would flag 'our_verdict does not match last verdict_history.to value' for all four.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: The current our_verdict and the current verdict_history last-entry disagree for all four predictions. our_verdict_prev is also stale (still points to the pre-2026-04-14 value for each). Recommend the decider fix all four in one pass and append new verdict_history entries documenting the restore.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: These are prediction-list entries, not WINs with monitor.py automation. monitor.py has no fetch logic for 'aetheric gravity wave speed', 'Schumann splitting Δf', 'NMP drift direction dominance', or 'Roaring 40s 500 hPa anomaly threshold'. Author status updates are hand-edited by the dome author.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: PRED-NEW-002 relabels P-wave seismology as 'aetheric gravity wave'. PRED-TOROID-003 relabels spherical-cavity Schumann splitting as 'ovoid eccentricity splitting'. W019 relabels core-mantle flux dynamics (covered by WIN-043). W020 describes a Ferrel-cell feature (Roaring 40s) and correlates it with SAA geometry (covered by WIN-024 Misleading). All four are standard-physics relabels.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: All four have data_predates_registration=true or equivalent: W019 and W020 were registered 2026-03-12 for phenomena observed for decades; PRED-NEW-002 cites a seismic velocity within a long-established range; PRED-TOROID-003 restates WIN-038 framework (which itself is post-hoc relabeling of 1952 Schumann work).
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: PRED-TOROID-003: EXP-136 shows the derivation path from dome eccentricity to 0.336 Hz is internally inconsistent (eccentricity 6.6x off; uses globe 7.83 Hz). PRED-NEW-002: no derivation from dome geometry — the 9.2 km/s number is asserted. W019 and W020: no dome-geometry derivation; both are observation-adoption.

holes_found.description: Commit 209fda5 (decider self-apply, 2026-04-18) reverted four verdicts that were explicitly human-approved on 2026-04-14 via ISS-951/952/953/954, without re-running through human approval and without recording the change in verdict_history. The analyst assessments used (dated 2026-04-11) are THE SAME assessments that were over-ruled by the original human approval, so no new reasoning justifies the revert. This is a workflow regression that undermines the integrity of the 'human approves verdict changes' rule.
holes_found.recommendation: Restore the 2026-04-14 human-approved verdicts: W019=falsified, W020=falsified, PRED-NEW-002=standard_physics, PRED-TOROID-003=standard_physics. Append a new verdict_history entry to each documenting the restore ('restored to human-approved value; silent revert in 209fda5 did not have human approval'). Open a decider issue (or human note) to cover-plate the root cause: analyst assessments older than the latest closed verdict-change issue for that prediction must not be auto-integrated without cross-checking closed-issues.json.

holes_found.description: verdict_history integrity broken for all four predictions. For each, the last verdict_history entry's 'to' value does NOT match the current our_verdict, and no new entry documents the revert. Similarly our_verdict_prev is stale (still reflects the pre-2026-04-14 value, not the 2026-04-14 human-approved value). Any downstream agent or integrity check that assumes verdict_history is complete will silently miss this state.
holes_found.recommendation: Fix as part of the restore in the previous hole. Decider should also consider a pre-push integrity gate: 'assert our_verdict matches last verdict_history.to; if not, error.' Tinker should audit whether any other predictions have this inconsistency.

holes_found.description: PRED-TOROID-003 entry is internally contradictory between fields: tldr asserts the derivation is broken and 'matches standard spherical cavity asymmetry' (i.e., the standard_physics framing), while our_verdict is 'unfalsifiable'. detail_reasoning concludes 'kernel of truth: Schumann splitting IS real and IS caused by cavity asymmetry' — again consistent with standard_physics — yet verdict is unfalsifiable. Readers who compare the tldr to the verdict tag will see an inconsistency similar to the ones we flag in dome WINs (e.g., WIN-012 κ denominator). Do not let our own review fall into the same trap we criticize.
holes_found.recommendation: When verdicts are restored per the previous hole, this resolves automatically (tldr and detail_reasoning already align with standard_physics). If the decider wants to keep unfalsifiable, the tldr and detail_reasoning MUST be rewritten to match — you cannot have the evidence saying 'standard physics explains this' and the verdict saying 'untestable'.

holes_found.description: PRED-NEW-002 entry's 'notes' field still contains the 2026-04-11 decider commentary explaining why standard_physics is more accurate than unfalsifiable — but the current our_verdict is unfalsifiable. The notes literally argue against the current verdict: 'calling it unfalsifiable gives the dome an unearned grievance.' Anyone reading the entry top-to-bottom will see the reasoning contradicting the verdict.
holes_found.recommendation: If verdict is restored to standard_physics (recommended), notes remain internally consistent and need no change. If for some reason unfalsifiable is retained, notes must be rewritten to remove the now-contradictory commentary.

holes_found.description: The queue item 185 was pushed 2026-04-18T17:28Z — more than 42 hours before this review. During that time the regression has been live in data/predictions.json, meaning the published site has been showing softened verdicts for four predictions where the analysis text and the verdict tag disagree. Low blast radius (predictions.json is not the top-summary tally) but worth confirming no external reader has cited the softened verdict in the intervening window.
holes_found.recommendation: When restoring, note the window of regression in the commit message for traceability. Consider a poller-level spot-check: did the dome author notice any verdict softening in our review during this window and incorporate it into his own narrative (e.g., 'funwithscience-org agrees with us on W019')?

holes_found.description: The analyst assessment files (monitor/analyst/expansions/PRED-assessment-*.json) are dated 2026-04-11 and remain unchanged despite the subsequent human approval overriding them on 2026-04-14. They are the stale source of truth that the decider re-integrated. The assessments should have been either updated in-place (with a note appended acknowledging the human decision) or explicitly marked superseded. Currently they function as a hidden land mine: any future re-integration run will trigger the same regression.
holes_found.recommendation: Analyst should update assessment files (or append supersedes_notes) whenever a verdict they proposed is over-ruled by human approval. Alternatively, decider's integration pass should check closed-issues.json for the prediction_id and skip integration when a later human-approved verdict exists. Suggest opening an issue for the workflow fix.

stronger_arguments.description: The clearest framing of the regression is 'stale analyst work overwrote fresh human decisions'. This is a common pipeline failure mode and should be documented as a known anti-pattern in the decider/analyst prompts. Surface it as an integrity invariant: last-closed verdict-change issue > older analyst assessment.
sources: Current predictions.json entries showing our_verdict != verdict_history last 'to' value

stronger_arguments.description: For the PRED-TOROID-003 case specifically, consider upgrading the preferred verdict from standard_physics to recycled. The claim is tagged restates_win=WIN-038, and WIN-038 is Self-Contradicted (dome predicts ~22 Hz but observes 7.83 Hz). A prediction that recycles a self-contradicted WIN is cleanly 'recycled' — possibly the strongest one-word verdict. standard_physics is the minimum defensible call; recycled is strictly tighter. Leave as standard_physics per human approval unless a new issue is opened.

For W019, the stronger framing is 'doubly-failed': (1) the specific directional prediction (poleward dominance) is falsified by observation; (2) even if one tried to rescue W019 as 'NMP drift happens at all', it duplicates WIN-043 which is already Std Model Explains. So W019 is either falsified OR redundant with WIN-043 — both verdicts are unfavorable to the dome, and unfalsifiable/standard_physics both miss this.
sources: WIN-043 (NMP drift 2.26x longitudinal, verdict Std Model Explains)
Author REFINED status on W019 (dome site self-report)

kernel_of_truth.description: The decider's commit did legitimately re-integrate analyst assessments that existed in the filesystem — the workflow literally tells the decider to read analyst assessments and apply them. The analyst's underlying point across all four — that some of these claims edge toward unfalsifiability in the absence of a defined detection protocol — is not wrong as a general observation about the dome's prediction style.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: General observations about dome prediction style do NOT warrant reverting specific human-approved verdicts. The analyst's 2026-04-11 framing was considered, debated via ISS-951/952/953/954, and explicitly over-ruled. Re-applying it later without a new human approval is not 'following the workflow' — it is a gap IN the workflow that needs to be closed. And the defender counter-arguments inspected in the per-prediction section all fail on inspection; none of the four softenings is individually defensible on the merits.

other_side_consideration.description: Per user preference, I steel-manned the case for keeping the current softened verdicts. The strongest version of that case is: (1) Verdicts are re-assessable at any time; rigid 'human approval locks forever' is bad process. (2) The dome's 'aetheric' framing genuinely is hard to falsify with seismic data; unfalsifiable is a reasonable conservative call. (3) W020's test window is nominally 'ongoing' with no close date; falsified before window close is premature. (4) The Roaring 40s 3% threshold is under-specified in averaging method; under-specified criterion supports unfalsifiable over falsified.
other_side_consideration.why_still_reject: (1) Verdicts ARE re-assessable — but the mechanism is 'open a new issue, get new human approval', not 'silent revert in a self-apply commit that doesn't even update verdict_history'. (2) The 'aetheric vs seismic' category objection was considered in ISS-953 and rejected; nothing new supports re-raising it. (3) Ongoing-window-never-closes is the exact 'unfalsifiable shield' pattern we criticize in dome-author process; we should not import it into our own scoring. (4) Under-specified criterion + author concession of failure = falsified with a margin-of-error note, not unfalsifiable. Each side's argument was weighed in the original ISSes and lost; rerunning the same argument with no new evidence is not a defense, it is repetition.

deeper_analysis: This review is as much about pipeline governance as it is about four verdict tags. The regression pattern (stale analyst assessments silently overwriting human-approved decisions) is a class-of-bug that almost certainly affects or will affect other data. Recommend the tinker agent audit: (a) every prediction entry where our_verdict != verdict_history[last].to; (b) every closed verdict-change issue where the current value in data/ does not match the issue's resolution; (c) add a decider pre-push integrity gate that blocks a verdict change if a closed issue within the last 30 days set a different value for the same prediction_id/win_id. The single-run fix is to restore the four verdicts; the systemic fix is to close the integration-vs-human-approval race.

citations_verified: ISS-951 in monitor/decisions/closed-issues.json, fixed_by: human-decision, resolution approved falsified for W019
ISS-952 in monitor/decisions/closed-issues.json, fixed_by: human-decision, resolution approved falsified for W020
ISS-953 in monitor/decisions/closed-issues.json, fixed_by: human-decision, resolution approved standard_physics for PRED-NEW-002
ISS-954 in monitor/decisions/closed-issues.json, fixed_by: human-decision, resolution approved standard_physics for PRED-TOROID-003
Commit 209fda5 shows the four verdict reversions in data/predictions.json as a unified diff
WIN-038 verdict=Self-Contradicted in data/wins.json (dome predicts ~22 Hz, observes 7.83 Hz)
WIN-043 verdict=Std Model Explains in data/wins.json (NMP drift 2.26x longitudinal)
WIN-024 verdict=Misleading in data/wins.json (Roaring 40s = SAA boundary)
citations_unchecked: Nickolaenko & Hayakawa 2002 (Schumann splitting 0.1–0.3 Hz observed range) — cited in analyst detail_reasoning; not independently re-verified here
PREM model P-wave velocity 8–13 km/s range — cited in ISS-953; not independently re-verified here
